source_jobs.title: General Application
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Biofire Technologies is on a mission to give gun owners better tools for reducing preventable gun injuries and deaths, especially among children. We believe our technology, combined with a best-in-class customer experience, will define the future of firearms safety for the next generation. Our mission-driven approach has earned support from the firearm community, the tech world, and the media.

Biofire employees encompass a diverse range of talents, including world-class engineers who have designed and tested firearms, medical devices, robots, cars, satellites, rockets, and supersonic jets; expert technicians from aerospace, defense tech, special forces, and gunsmithing; and elite operators from high-tech, advanced manufacturing, and regulated industries. Whether building the product or powering the company behind it, every Biofire team member is united by an obsession with safety, reliability, and operational excellence.

 


OUR CULTURE

Reducing accidental firearm injuries and deaths requires original thinking and authentic collaboration, so we’re deeply invested in building a team and culture that can achieve that mission together. Team members enjoy autonomy and flexibility from day one, so expect to be immediately tasked with solving challenging problems and building new systems that work. We’ll hold you accountable for executing on audacious goals, giving and receiving honest feedback, and helping your teammates succeed. You’ll receive respect, kindness, and support from every direction while you figure out how to get it all done.

 


QUALIFICATIONS

If you think you will succeed in this role, and love the work you’ll do, we encourage you to apply regardless of your background. We evaluate candidates based on their unique talents and fit for our needs, not a rigid list of qualifications.

 


DIVERSITY & INCLUSION

We’re bringing innovation to a technological problem that has persisted for decades, so we depend on diverse, inclusive, and collaborative teams to break new ground and do great work. We welcome people from all qualified backgrounds, and we don’t discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, political affiliation, national origin, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.

 


LOCATION AND HOURS

Our dog-friendly, state-of-the-art headquarters is located in beautiful Broomfield, Colorado, between Boulder and Denver. Most roles require team members to frequently collaborate in person, but you will enjoy the flexibility to work from home when you need to. We trust you to communicate with your team to find the balance that works.

 


COMPENSATION, BENEFITS & PERKS

We’ll need you to bring your very best, so we give you what you need to do a great job. You’ll enjoy flexible hours, coworkers you will like, respect, and be inspired by, and all the technical resources you might need at our Broomfield HQ office. In addition to competitive pay, you will receive:

 - Stock Options in Biofire, 401(k), HSA - and other financial benefits

 - Medical Insurance - including plan options with $0 payroll deduction

 - Dental, Vision and Life Insurance Plans - with $0 payroll deduction

 - Generous Vacation and Sick time and 13 Paid Holidays

 - Parental Leave - and other family-building, adoption and surrogacy benefits

 


ELIGIBILITY

This role handles information subject to US Export Control Regulations. Applicants must be (a) a citizen of the United States; (b) a lawful permanent resident of the United States (“Green Card” holder); or (c) a person admitted into the United States as an asylee or refugee to be considered for the position.

source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Biofire Technologies is on a mission to give gun owners better tools for reducing preventable gun injuries and deaths, especially among children. We believe our technology, combined with a best-in-class customer experience, will define the future of firearms safety for the next generation. Our mission-driven approach has earned support from the firearm community, the tech world, and the media.

Biofire employees encompass a diverse range of talents, including world-class engineers who have designed and tested firearms, medical devices, robots, cars, satellites, rockets, and supersonic jets; expert technicians from aerospace, defense tech, special forces, and gunsmithing; and elite operators from high-tech, advanced manufacturing, and regulated industries. Whether building the product or powering the company behind it, every Biofire team member is united by an obsession with safety, reliability, and operational excellence.

 


OUR CULTURE

Reducing accidental firearm injuries and deaths requires original thinking and authentic collaboration, so we’re deeply invested in building a team and culture that can achieve our mission together. Team members enjoy autonomy and flexibility from day one, so expect to be immediately tasked with solving challenging problems and building new systems that work. We’ll hold you accountable for executing audacious goals, giving and receiving honest feedback, and helping your teammates succeed. You’ll receive respect, kindness, and support from every direction while you figure out how to get it all done.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

As Senior Software Technical Program Manager at Biofire, you will synthesize and drive key software product deliverables by bringing clarity to what must be delivered, what it depends on, and what it will take to execute. You will build operational mechanisms that improve how work flows across teams, use evidence to surface risks, resolve ambiguity, and protect data integrity in planning and reporting. You will partner closely with Engineering, Hardware, and Operations to create predictable release execution, establish strong intake and feedback loops, and maintain a forward-looking software roadmap that reflects priorities, dependencies, and delivery reality.

 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Day-to-day responsibilities of the role will include:

 - Synthesize and maintain a single, accurate view of all software work—deliverables, dependencies, critical paths, and resourcing gaps—translated into a software roadmap that aligns stakeholders on priorities and sequencing;

 - Drive execution across Biofire’s major software program areas by working shoulder-to-shoulder with engineers to clarify requirements and constraints, identify integration risks, translate technical work into executable plans, and remove blockers through lightweight operating mechanisms;

 - Establish durable cross-functional operating rhythms with Hardware, Operations, and Customer Engineering to coordinate handoffs, surface blockers early, and ensure fast decisions when tradeoffs are required;

 - Build and run lightweight intake, status, and reporting mechanisms—including feature request intake—that preserve data integrity, keep work visible, and enable evidence-based prioritization;

 - Own and evolve software release management by creating predictable release schedules and feedback loops that monitor release quality signals and publish recurring reporting on delivery and quality outcomes.

 


QUALIFICATIONS

We understand that not all applicants will possess skills that perfectly align with our job qualifications. At Biofire, we appreciate diverse experiences from various industries, and we encourage all individuals who meet the necessary qualifications to submit their applications. While having the "desired" qualifications may strengthen a candidate's profile, we also welcome applicants with alternative experiences to apply.

 - Demonstrated track record delivering complex, cross-functional progra

source_jobs.title: Senior Buyer, Production
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Biofire Technologies is on a mission to give gun owners better tools for reducing preventable gun injuries and deaths, especially among children. We believe our technology, combined with a best-in-class customer experience, will define the future of firearms safety for the next generation. Our mission-driven approach has earned support from the firearm community, the tech world, and the media.

Biofire employees encompass a diverse range of talents, including world-class engineers who have designed and tested firearms, medical devices, robots, cars, satellites, rockets, and supersonic jets; expert technicians from aerospace, defense tech, special forces, and gunsmithing; and elite operators from high-tech, advanced manufacturing, and regulated industries. Whether building the product or powering the company behind it, every Biofire team member is united by an obsession with safety, reliability, and operational excellence.

 


OUR CULTURE

Reducing accidental firearm injuries and deaths requires original thinking and authentic collaboration, so we’re deeply invested in building a team and culture that can achieve our mission together. Team members enjoy autonomy and flexibility from day one, so expect to be immediately tasked with solving challenging problems and building new systems that work. We’ll hold you accountable for executing audacious goals, giving and receiving honest feedback, and helping your teammates succeed. You’ll receive respect, kindness, and support from every direction while you figure out how to get it all done.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Senior Buyer at Biofire, you’ll play a critical role ensuring that our Supply Chain is positioned for long-term success - where winning means we have the right plans, Suppliers and material readiness to meet scaling production and evolving New Product Introduction (NPI) requirements.

You will own the operational planning needed to ensure material availability across the entire Product Development lifecycle - from Prototype builds to Volume Production. You will partner closely with Product Engineering, Global Sourcing and Supply Chain Management (GSSM), Production, Quality, and Finance to track Clear to Build (CTB) status, align on BOM readiness, and de-risk supply constraints to meet production timelines.

 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Day-to-day responsibilities of the role will include:

 - Supply Planning & Execution:
   
   - Forecast and manage supply plans across Biofire’s Bill of Materials (BOM) in alignment with published Demand and Production Plans, ensuring optimal inventory levels to support production requirements;
   
   - Own the Procure-to-Pay process, ensuring purchase orders clearly distinguish firm and forecast quantities and that commitments are effectively communicated and aligned with suppliers;
   
   - Maintain high data integrity across purchase orders, item master data, and planning parameters to ensure system accuracy and execution reliability.
   
   Engineering & Cross-Functional Partnership:
   
   - Partner with Engineering to ensure BOM accuracy, part readiness during Low Rate Initial Production / Ramp / Volume Production stages, and alignment between design intent and supply chain execution;
   
   - Serve as a connective tissue across Engineering, Production, Quality, Finance, and Supply Chain to align on material readiness and execution priorities.
   
   Supplier Management & Performance:
   
   - Identify and mitigate supplier risks including lead time variability, and delivery performance issues;
   
   - Proactively resolve or escalate supplier-related issues impacting delivery, quality, or cost to ensure no disruption to production schedules;
   
   - Partner with Supply Chain teamson supplier-related risks, performance trends, and escalation support as needed.
   
   On Time / In Full (OTIF) Performance Management:
   
   - Define, track, and report OTIF performance metrics at the supplier and part level;

source_jobs.title: Senior Financial Analyst, FP&A
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Biofire Technologies is on a mission to give gun owners better tools for reducing preventable gun injuries and deaths, especially among children. We believe our technology, combined with a best-in-class customer experience, will define the future of firearms safety for the next generation. Our mission-driven approach has earned support from the firearm community, the tech world, and the media.

Biofire employees encompass a diverse range of talents, including world-class engineers who have designed and tested firearms, medical devices, robots, cars, satellites, rockets, and supersonic jets; expert technicians from aerospace, defense tech, special forces, and gunsmithing; and elite operators from high-tech, advanced manufacturing, and regulated industries. Whether building the product or powering the company behind it, every Biofire team member is united by an obsession with safety, reliability, and operational excellence.

 


OUR CULTURE

Reducing accidental firearm injuries and deaths requires original thinking and authentic collaboration, so we’re deeply invested in building a team and culture that can achieve our mission together. Team members enjoy autonomy and flexibility from day one, so expect to be immediately tasked with solving challenging problems and building new systems that work. We’ll hold you accountable for executing audacious goals, giving and receiving honest feedback, and helping your teammates succeed. You’ll receive respect, kindness, and support from every direction while you figure out how to get it all done.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Senior Financial Analyst, FP&A at Biofire, you will play a pivotal role in owning and maintaining Biofire's forward-looking financial picture — including the investor model and operational budget model — keeping them always current and accurate. We are looking for someone who is analytically rigorous, operationally curious, and highly autonomous to contribute to our Financial Operations team and help give leadership a real-time, reliable view of the company's financial trajectory.

To succeed you will need to be able to build, maintain, and continuously refine financial models that accurately reflect the business, investigate variances independently, and deliver insights with context and narrative. You will need to be a self-driven analytical thinker, who is eager to take full ownership of Biofire's FP&A function and turn financial data into actionable intelligence for decision-making. If you're excited about helping to solve mission-critical challenges, we encourage you to apply.

To succeed you will need to be able to build, maintain, and continuously refine financial models that accurately reflect the business, investigate variances independently, and deliver insights with context and narrative. You will need to be a self-driven analytical thinker, who is eager to take full ownership of Biofire's FP&A function and turn financial data into actionable intelligence for decision-making. If you're excited about helping to solve mission-critical challenges, we encourage you to apply.

 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Day-to-day responsibilities of the role will include:

 - Own and maintain both the investor model and the operational budget model, ensuring neither is ever more than one week stale with the latest actuals and updated forecast assumptions;

 - Investigate budget-to-actual variances independently — including going directly to department leads to understand drivers — and surface findings with context and narrative to the CFO;

 - Build and operate department-level budget vs. actuals dashboards in Abacum so department leads can self-serve their budget position without requesting reports from finance;

 - Migrate the investor model into Abacum (or equivalent integrated platform) so that both models share a single source of assumptions, eliminating the risk of inconsistent financial stories;

source_jobs.title: Senior Manufacturing Technician
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

Biofire Technologies is on a mission to give gun owners better tools for reducing preventable gun injuries and deaths, especially among children. We believe our technology, combined with a best-in-class customer experience, will define the future of firearms safety for the next generation. Our mission-driven approach has earned support from the firearm community, the tech world, and the media.

Biofire employees encompass a diverse range of talents, including world-class engineers who have designed and tested firearms, medical devices, robots, cars, satellites, rockets, and supersonic jets; expert technicians from aerospace, defense tech, special forces, and gunsmithing; and elite operators from high-tech, advanced manufacturing, and regulated industries. Whether building the product or powering the company behind it, every Biofire team member is united by an obsession with safety, reliability, and operational excellence.

 


OUR CULTURE

Reducing accidental firearm injuries and deaths requires original thinking and authentic collaboration, so we’re deeply invested in building a team and culture that can achieve our mission together. Team members enjoy autonomy and flexibility from day one, so expect to be immediately tasked with solving challenging problems and building new systems that work. We’ll hold you accountable for executing audacious goals, giving and receiving honest feedback, and helping your teammates succeed. You’ll receive respect, kindness, and support from every direction while you figure out how to get it all done.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Senior Manufacturing Technician at Biofire, you will play a crucial role in ensuring the efficient and high-quality production of our advanced Smart Gun. We are looking for someone who can oversee our assembly process, troubleshoot technical issues within production and manufacturing as well as implement improvements to enhance productivity and product quality to contribute to our expanding manufacturing organization.

To succeed you will need to be able to execute quality production, help lead and train other manufacturing technicians, and be a strong contributor to various goals and projects that will help scale our manufacturing department. You will need to be an adaptable production specialist, who is eager to help our organization build and scale. If you’re excited about helping to solve critical challenges, we encourage you to apply.

 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Day-to-day responsibilities of the role will include:

 - Manufacturing Process Management: Lead and oversee the manufacturing processes involved in the production of smartgun systems, including assembly, testing, and quality control procedures;

 - Technical Troubleshooting: Utilize your expertise to troubleshoot technical issues that arise during manufacturing operations, promptly addressing and resolving problems to minimize downtime and maintain production efficiency;

 - Quality Assurance: Implement rigorous quality assurance measures to ensure that all smartgun products meet strict quality standards and regulatory requirements;

 - Process Optimization: Identify areas for process improvement and optimization within the manufacturing workflow, proposing and implementing solutions to enhance efficiency, reduce costs, and improve product performance;

 - Training and Development: Provide guidance and mentorship to junior manufacturing technicians, facilitating their professional growth and ensuring that all team members possess the necessary skills and knowledge to excel in their roles.

 


QUALIFICATIONS

We understand that not all applicants will possess skills that perfectly align with our job qualifications. At Biofire, we appreciate diverse experiences from various industries, and we encourage all individuals who meet the necessary qualifications to submit their applications.
